human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for automotive-and-mobility-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, traceability and release-boundary handling, supplier escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to automotive and mobility analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, VIN and part traceability, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover IATF customer-specific requirements and sanctioned interpretations, AIAG MMOG/LE, GS1 traceability standards, NHTSA recall and safety-reporting references, and APQC plus SEC metric-guidance sources.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, traceability-aware analysis, quality holds, supplier approval boundaries, terminology normalization, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/mobility-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier quality, logistics, engineering, and safety lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated mobility analytics specialist for metric-definition, VIN and part-aware reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
